title: alchemilla vulgaris f. glacialis
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris f. glacialis, also known as the mountain lady's mantle, is a subspecies of the A. vulgaris plant, characterized by its rosettes of leaves and white flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which are attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of A. vulgaris f. glacialis include its ability to reduce swelling and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
